title: pentas lanceolata subsp. lanceolata
intro: Pentas lanceolata subsp. lanceolata, also known as East African pentas, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to eastern Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of East African pentas include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
